blurb: Apple services security encompasses the authentication, authorization, and encryption protections applied to iCloud, Apple Account, iMessage, FaceTime, Apple Pay, and other Apple-operated services. All service communications use TLS with certificate pinning to prevent interception. Apple Account is protected by two-factor authentication using hardware-rooted device trust. iCloud data is encrypted at rest in Apple's data centers, with end-to-end encrypted categories – expanded by Advanced Data Protection – where Apple holds no decryption keys. CloudKit end-to-end encryption uses per-record symmetric keys wrapped with device-specific keys, ensuring only the user's enrolled devices can decrypt their data.
